text: 33.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
1.3g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for dairy, the best versions keep the convenience without wrecking the fat profile.
24.9g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
vitamin b12 at 30% daily value, vitamin a at 30% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
calcium at 72% daily value, zinc at 21% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

the upsides first: very strong calcium identity. this is one of the cleanest reasons cheese still performs well inside dairy despite its drawbacks. protein density is strong. cheddar clearly still delivers on the protein side of dairy. very practical and versatile. it is genuinely easy to use in lots of foods.

on the weaker side: saturated fat is a major drawback. this is the biggest reason cheddar is not an easy elite dairy food. energy density is harsh. calories add up quickly compared with cleaner dairy anchors. easy to overeat. highly palatable and dense enough to add up fast.
B tier. Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
